demographics.living_situation: lives at home in a private residence

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: There's a constant, heavy pressure under my right ribs, I'm exhausted all the time, and after a blood test came back abnormal I'm terrified my liver is failing or there's a tumour.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The dull ache under my right ribs has gone from a vague niggle to a constant heavy ache over the last two days, and I'm frightened because my GP said my recent liver blood test was off.

symptom_narrative.onset: the vague discomfort started about 3 months ago; it acutely worsened over the past 48 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: 3 months of niggling discomfort, now a constant heavy ache for 2 days
symptom_narrative.character: a dull, heavy, pressure-like ache — not sharp and not coming in waves
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: not agony, but constant and dragging, and it's wearing me down on top of the tiredness
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: up under the right ribs, on the right side of my upper belly
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there all the time now rather than coming and going
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much seems to change it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: nothing specific — it just sits there
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I've been really wiped out and lethargic for months; no fevers, no shivers, no vomiting, no throwing up blood, my bowel motions look normal to me and my skin and eyes don't look yellow that I can tell
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before — it's the constancy over the last two days that scared me
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can still get around and do things, but the fatigue really limits how much I can exert myself

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: type 2 diabetes, and I know my sugars have been running high and not well controlled
high cholesterol / fats in the blood
I carry a lot of weight around my middle and I've been told I have metabolic syndrome

social_history_volunteered.alcohol: I hardly drink at all — no real alcohol to speak of
social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: I'm pretty sedentary and I drink a fair few sugary soft drinks through the day
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: no history of hepatitis or blood-borne virus risks that I'm aware of

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants firm reassurance that he does not have liver failure or a liver tumour

objective_data_offered.value: my home blood pressure monitor read about 155 over 95 this morning
I weighed about 115 kg on my bathroom scales
you can see I carry a lot of weight around my middle; the whites of my eyes look white to me, not yellow
I've noticed dark, velvety-looking patches of skin on the back of my neck and under my arms
